days.title: Arrival in Paris
days.subtitle: First day exploring the City of Lights

timeline.title: Arrive at Gare du Nord
timeline.detail: After taking the Eurostar from London, arrive at Gare du Nord station, follow signs to the exit and take a taxi or metro to your hotel.

timeline.title: Check in to Hôtel de la Bourdonnais
timeline.detail: Check into this charming hotel located just a short walk from the Eiffel Tower. A perfect base for exploring the city.

timeline.title: Lunch at Café de Flore
timeline.detail: Enjoy a classic Parisian lunch at this iconic café. Try a Croque Monsieur with a side of French fries while soaking up the café's historical ambience.

timeline.title: Visit the Eiffel Tower
timeline.detail: Head to the Eiffel Tower for breathtaking views over the city. Make sure to buy tickets in advance online to avoid waiting in lengthy lines.

timeline.title: Seine Riverboat Tour
timeline.detail: Take a scenic boat tour along the Seine River to see iconic landmarks from the water, including Notre Dame and the Louvre.

timeline.title: Dinner at Le Procope
timeline.detail: Dine at the oldest café in Paris, famous for its historical charm and traditional French cuisine. Don't miss their Coq au Vin.

localTips: Consider buying a museum pass for easier access to popular sites.
Always check for online ticket deals for major attractions.
Learn a few basic French phrases, it's appreciated by locals.

days.title: Exploring Paris Museums and Culture
days.subtitle: A day filled with art and history

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a complimentary French breakfast at the hotel, including croissants, coffee, and fresh fruit to fuel your day.

timeline.title: Visit the Louvre Museum
timeline.detail: Explore the world-famous Louvre Museum, home to thousands of artworks, including the Mona Lisa and the Venus de Milo. Pre-book tickets to skip the line.

timeline.title: Lunch at Le Fumoir
timeline.detail: Savor a leisurely lunch at this stylish restaurant situated near the Louvre, known for its classic French dishes and chic atmosphere.

timeline.title: Visit Musée d'Orsay
timeline.detail: Visit this stunning museum housed in a former railway station, showcasing an extensive collection of Impressionist and Post-Impressionist masterpieces.

timeline.title: Montmartre Evening Stroll
timeline.detail: Wander through the artistic streets of Montmartre, visit the Basilica of Sacré-Cœur, and enjoy the sunset views of the city skyline.

timeline.title: Dinner at La Maison Rose
timeline.detail: Dine at this picturesque pink restaurant in Montmartre, famous for its quaint charm and delicious traditional dishes. Order the beef stew!

localTips: Purchase a combined ticket for the Louvre and Musée d'Orsay for convenience.
Check museum hours in advance, as they may vary.
Walk to Montmartre; the streets are beautiful, especially at dusk.

days.title: Last Day in Paris
days.subtitle: Final explorations and relaxation

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hôtel
timeline.detail: Indulge in a delightful breakfast at the hotel before checking out, featuring pastries, juices, and fresh coffee.

timeline.title: Visit Notre-Dame Cathedral
timeline.detail: Explore the iconic Notre-Dame Cathedral. Although under restoration, the exterior façade is still breathtaking and worth the visit.

timeline.title: Lunch at Le Relais de l'Entrecôte
timeline.detail: Enjoy a simple yet delicious meal at this popular spot, famous for steak-frites and their secret sauce. No menu, just a great meal!

timeline.title: Shopping at Le Marais
timeline.detail: Spend your afternoon shopping in the trendy Le Marais district, filled with boutiques, art galleries, and gourmet shops.

timeline.title: Return to Gare du Nord
timeline.detail: Head back to Gare du Nord for your return train to London. Keep your passport handy and check train times in advance.

timeline.title: Depart to London
timeline.detail: Board the Eurostar train for your return journey to London, reflecting on the beautiful memories made in Paris.

localTips: Consider buying gifts or souvenirs from local artists in Le Marais.
Plan to arrive at Gare du Nord at least 30 minutes before departure.
If you have time, try a last pastry from a local boulangerie.

hotels.name: Hôtel de la Bourdonnais
hotels.note: Located steps from the Eiffel Tower, providing a cozy ambiance.
hotels.address: 111 Avenue de la Bourdonnais, 75007 Paris, France

budget.note: Return journey from London to Paris

urgent.label: Book Louvre and Musée d'Orsay Tickets
urgent.note: Avoid long queues; book ahead online.

urgent.label: Reserve Seine Riverboat Tour
urgent.note: Secure your spot for the scenic tour.
